question: Create a `ResourceHandler` class that manages a dynamically allocated integer array. Its constructor should allocate an array of a specified size. Implement a destructor that correctly deallocates the dynamically allocated memory to prevent memory leaks.

test_cases.expected:
Creating handler1 with size 3
handler1 array: [10, 20, 30]
Creating handler2 as a copy of handler1 (shallow copy)
handler2 array: [10, 20, 30]
Modifying handler1...
handler1 array after modification: [99, 20, 30]
handler2 array (should also reflect change due to shallow copy): [99, 20, 30]
handler1 and handler2 now point to the same memory.
When handler1 is destroyed, it will deallocate the memory.
When handler2 is destroyed, it will attempt to deallocate the same memory again (double-free).
End of test case. Destructors will be called.

errors: Missing copy constructor and copy assignment operator leads to shallow copy and potential double-free errors, violating the Rule of Three/Five. This makes the class non-robust when objects are copied.
